The '''Mission to Kuras Drift''' was a joint operation by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] to resupply the its [[Airam sector/Legends|sector]] fleet with a new shipment of [[Warhead/Legends|warheads]].

==The Mission==
With rebel forces in the Airam sector gradually running low on warheads, Tamaron's offer of resupply was eagerly accepted. A small team of two [[Cargo ferry|cargo ferries]] together with the [[MC40a light cruiser/Legends|light Calamari Cruiser]] [[Condor (MC40a light cruiser)|''Condor'']] was set up to receive the shipment at the asteroid base ''Attila'' in the [[The Drift/Legends|Kuras Drift]].

Tamaron suspected that rival clan leader [[Ilay]] would not like this course of action, being an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]-friendly leader. He was proven correct when the resupply operation began.

While [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] from [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue]] and [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadrons]] maintained a patrol around the perimeter, inspecting the arriving freighters for accuracy of manifest, unexpected visitors turned up in form of [[R60 T-wing interceptor|T-wing]] flight Bravo. Despite numerous hails from the Airam base, the T-wings did not respond as they scanned the vicinity.

Suddenly, Bravo Flight engaged the Airam station, launching [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] at the facility before the rebel forces could react. Rogue and Gold Squadrons engaged the hostile starfighters and destroyed them quickly. Bravo reinforcements arriving on the scene were likewise shot down.

The situation was complicated further when Imperial [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]] entered the area and launched a warhead volley at ''Attila''. Amidst the chaos, the final expected Airam cargo ship, ''Lothar'', arrived—with the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Wasp (Imperial-class)|''Wasp'']] in hot pursuit.

The X-wings of Rogue and Gold Squadron managed to fend off the starfighter attacks on the facility and eventually knocked out the Star Destroyer's shields, forcing it into retreat. However, the relief came too late for the [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|corvette]] ''Lothar'', which succumbed to the warship's heavy [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries.

The Calamari Cruiser and the Alliance's cargo ferries managed to complete their cargo transfer operations and headed off into the safety of hyperspace before Imperial reinforcements could arrive.

==Aftermath==
With a fresh shipment of warheads, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] were now well prepared for further offensive action against [[Task Force Vengeance]]. However, the betrayal of [[Ilay]] revealed many of the Alliance's sensor probes in the sector to the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. The disabling or circumventing of this network left the Rebels blind to Imperial movements. Tamaron's spy in Ilay's pirate group provided information on the traitor, though, and Tamaron insisted that Ilay be captured. The Rebels conceded to their vital new ally, leading to the [[Battle of Aiqin 4]].

==Appearances==
